steps.title: Distribute $6xy$ across the parentheses
steps.content: Multiply each term inside by $6xy$: $$6xy \cdot 2x^2 = 12x^3 y \qquad \text{and} \qquad 6xy \cdot 5y = 30xy^2.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $x^3y + 11xy^2$ — missing the coefficient $12$ and incorrectly adds $12+30$ into $11xy^2$. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: $x^2y + 5y$ — ignores the $6xy$ factor entirely. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: $12x^2y + 30xy$ — wrong power of $x$ in the first term and wrong power of $y$ in the second. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: $12x^3y + 30xy^2$ — matches the distribution exactly. Correct.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
